annotator S.report_text:
APT29 has been observed crafting targeted spearphishing campaigns leveraging web links to a malicious dropper.
An adversary may rely upon a user clicking a malicious link in order to gain execution.
Once executed, the code delivers Remote Access Tools (RATs) and evades detection using a range of techniques.
An adversary may use legitimate desktop support and remote access software to establish an interactive command and control channel to target systems within networks.
Both groups exfiltrate and analyze information to gain intelligence value.
These actors set up operational infrastructure to obfuscate their source infrastructure, host domains and malware for targeting organizations, establish command and control nodes, and harvest credentials and other valuable information from their targets.
An APT29 spearphishing campaign directed emails containing a malicious link to over 1,000 recipients.
At least one targeted individual activated links to malware hosted on operational infrastructure or opened attachments containing malware.
APT29 delivered malware to the political party's systems.
APT29 conducted reconnaissance against Active Directory.
Exfiltrated email from several accounts.
Through encrypted connections back through operational infrastructure.

annotator L.report_text:
spearphishing campaigns leveraging web links to a malicious dropper APT29 created targeted spearphishing campaigns using links to a malicious dropper.

once executed, the code delivers Remote Access Tools (RATs) and evades detection using a range of techniques The dropper downloads and installs remote access tools (RATs) on the victim's machine.

These actors set up operational infrastructure to obfuscate their source infrastructure, host domains and malware.

APT29 spearphishing campaign directed emails containing a malicious link.
